category_rationale: Organization is the strongest single launch category for the MVP because it is broadly useful to busy professionals, easy to explain in one line, low-risk compared with advice-heavy categories, and naturally suited to concrete 5-15 minute actions that work well in email.

actions.title: Consolidate Sticky Notes
actions.instruction: Gather loose sticky notes or scraps of paper and copy every still-relevant item into one list.
actions.why_it_matters: One trusted list reduces repeated scanning and missed tasks.

actions.title: Clear One Counter Zone
actions.instruction: Choose one small section of a counter or table and clear, wipe, and reset only that area.
actions.why_it_matters: A cleaner surface lowers visual clutter and makes the space easier to use.

actions.title: Create an Action-Needed Label
actions.instruction: Create one email folder or label called Action Needed and move the next ten actionable messages into it.
actions.why_it_matters: Separating true action items from everything else makes follow-up easier.

actions.title: Sort One Small Drawer
actions.instruction: Empty one small drawer, throw away obvious trash, and put back only what belongs there.
actions.why_it_matters: Small storage spots create daily friction when they turn into mixed piles.

actions.title: Group Charging Cables
actions.instruction: Gather loose charging cables and place them together in one drawer, pouch, or container.
actions.why_it_matters: Keeping cables together cuts down on repeated searching.

actions.title: Archive Old Screenshots
actions.instruction: Delete or archive at least fifteen screenshots you no longer need.
actions.why_it_matters: A cleaner photo roll makes useful reference items faster to find.

actions.title: Make a Waiting-On List
actions.instruction: Write down up to five things you are waiting on from other people in one place.
actions.why_it_matters: Tracking dependencies prevents them from floating around in memory.

actions.title: Set a Return Spot
actions.instruction: Choose one visible place for items that need to be returned and move current return items there.
actions.why_it_matters: A dedicated return spot keeps errands from becoming scattered reminders.

actions.title: Merge Duplicate Contacts
actions.instruction: Merge or delete the obvious duplicate contacts in your phone until the list looks clean again.
actions.why_it_matters: A cleaner contact list makes the right person easier to find quickly.

actions.title: Build a Bills Folder
actions.instruction: Create one paper or digital folder for bills and statements that still need review.
actions.why_it_matters: Grouping time-sensitive paperwork makes it less likely to slip.

actions.title: Clear Desktop Icons
actions.instruction: Move stray files from your computer desktop into named folders or trash until only active items remain.
actions.why_it_matters: A cleaner desktop reduces visual noise when you start work.

actions.title: Stage the Entryway
actions.instruction: Reset the area by your door so your keys, shoes, or bag have a clear landing spot.
actions.why_it_matters: A stable entry point reduces small delays when leaving home.

actions.title: Rename Three Cloud Docs
actions.instruction: Rename three vague or untitled cloud documents so you can find them later.
actions.why_it_matters: Clear names save time the next time you need the file.

actions.title: Toss Expired Pantry Items
actions.instruction: Check one pantry or fridge shelf and discard expired items or move nearly expired items to the front.
actions.why_it_matters: A quick shelf review prevents waste and makes daily choices easier.

actions.title: Make a One-Trip Errands List
actions.instruction: Write one errands list from memory and group the items by the place where you would do them.
actions.why_it_matters: Bundled errands reduce repeat trips and forgotten stops.

actions.title: Reset Your Wallet
actions.instruction: Remove old receipts, cards, or notes from your wallet and keep only what you actually use.
actions.why_it_matters: A lean wallet is faster to use and easier to keep current.

actions.title: Set a Charging Station
actions.instruction: Choose one outlet or surface as your device charging station and place your main chargers there.
actions.why_it_matters: A fixed charging spot reduces daily hunting and cable drift.

actions.title: Collect Loose Mail
actions.instruction: Gather loose mail into one stack and separate it into action items, to-file items, and trash.
actions.why_it_matters: Sorting mail once is easier than re-reading the same pile.

actions.title: Create a Refill List
actions.instruction: Check two supplies you use often and write down anything that needs restocking.
actions.why_it_matters: A running refill list prevents avoidable last-minute shortages.

actions.title: Clear One App Badge
actions.instruction: Open one app with a large notification badge and clear, archive, or mute the obvious low-value items.
actions.why_it_matters: Fewer false badges make important notifications easier to notice.

actions.title: Scan Three Documents
actions.instruction: Scan or photograph three paper documents you may need later and save them in one named folder.
actions.why_it_matters: A small digital backup lowers the risk of losing important paper.

actions.title: Organize One Shelf
actions.instruction: Straighten one shelf and remove anything that belongs somewhere else.
actions.why_it_matters: A single tidy shelf creates an easy win and reduces visual drag.

actions.title: Make a Travel Essentials Pouch
actions.instruction: Pack the chargers, pens, or small items you keep repacking into one pouch you can grab quickly.
actions.why_it_matters: Grouped essentials reduce last-minute packing friction.

actions.title: Create a Weekend Drop Zone
actions.instruction: Place a box or basket where weekend clutter tends to collect and move current loose items into it.
actions.why_it_matters: A defined catch zone stops clutter from spreading across multiple surfaces.

actions.title: Review Subscription Renewals
actions.instruction: Check upcoming auto-renewals for the next 30 days and note any you want to revisit.
actions.why_it_matters: Looking ahead gives you time to make cleaner spending decisions.

actions.title: Clean Up Saved Links
actions.instruction: Delete or organize ten saved links, bookmarks, or read-later items you know you will not use.
actions.why_it_matters: A smaller backlog makes the remaining saves more credible and useful.

actions.title: Set a Laundry Finish Reminder
actions.instruction: Create one reminder for moving or folding laundry at the point you usually forget it.
actions.why_it_matters: A well-placed reminder prevents a predictable chore from stalling.

actions.title: Reset One Bag Pocket
actions.instruction: Empty one small pocket or compartment in a bag and put back only what belongs there.
actions.why_it_matters: Small storage pockets create friction fast when they fill with random items.

actions.title: Make a Morning Paper Stack
actions.instruction: Put the papers or printouts you need tomorrow into one stack you can grab in one move.
actions.why_it_matters: A prepared stack reduces next-day scrambling.

actions.title: Restore One Visible Zone
actions.instruction: Spend five minutes putting away whatever is out of place in the nearest visible zone around you.
actions.why_it_matters: A short visible reset creates immediate order when energy is low.
